subtasks.description: Query IAM system to retrieve all roles flagged as having elevated permissions, including their complete permission grant list and metadata (role name, created date, last modified).
Query audit/logging system to extract all permissions actually exercised by each IAM role in the last 180 days, aggregated by permission type and frequency of use.

Reconcile granted permissions (from sql_elevated_roles) against exercised permissions (from sql_exercised_perms). Identify permission gaps (granted but never used in 180 days). Classify each gap by risk severity (unused high-risk permissions, unused moderate permissions, etc.). Generate revocation candidates with rationale and confidence scoring.

plan.cost_rationale: Two independent SQL queries (mid tier each) gather granted and exercised permissions from separate sources with moderately complex filtering and aggregation. Cross-recon (frontier tier) performs the reconciliation logic: matching permissions across schemas, identifying gaps, classifying risk, and generating revocation recommendations with scoring. The frontier tier is justified because this reconciliation requires multi-signal analysis (permission severity, usage patterns, role context) and inference to classify candidates reliably. Single-subtask approach within each specialist avoids redundant calls: one sql_gen retrieves all elevated roles with their grants in one query; another sql_gen extracts all exercise logs with aggregation; cross_recon consolidates the gap analysis and classification in a single pass rather than splitting into separate policy or classification steps.

output.sql:
SELECT
    alp.permission AS granted_permission,
    alp.resource,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN alf.result = 'ALLOW' AND alf.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN alf.log_id END) AS times_exercised_last_180d,
    MAX(CASE WHEN alf.result = 'ALLOW' AND alf.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN alf.access_time END) AS last_exercised_at,
    COUNT(DISTINCT alp.user_id) AS users_with_permission,
    CASE
        WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN alf.result = 'ALLOW' AND alf.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' THEN alf.log_id END) = 0
        THEN 'REVOCATION_CANDIDATE'
        ELSE 'IN_USE'
    END AS revocation_flag
FROM (
    -- Simulate IAM role grants: distinct user+permission+resource combos as granted permissions
    SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id,
        permission,
        resource
    FROM access_log_facts
    WHERE permission IN (
        -- Elevated permissions heuristic: admin, write, delete, manage actions
        SELECT DISTINCT permission
        FROM access_log_facts
        WHERE permission ILIKE '%admin%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%write%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%delete%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%manage%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%superuser%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%elevated%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%root%'
           OR permission ILIKE '%owner%'
    )
) alp
LEFT JOIN access_log_facts alf
    ON  alp.user_id    = alf.user_id
    AND alp.permission = alf.permission
    AND alp.resource   = alf.resource
    AND alf.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'
    AND alf.result = 'ALLOW'
GROUP BY
    alp.permission,
    alp.resource
ORDER BY
    revocation_flag DESC,
    times_exercised_last_180d ASC,
    alp.permission,
    alp.resource

SELECT
    a.permission,
    a.action,
    a.result,
    COUNT(*) AS usage_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.user_id) AS distinct_users,
    MIN(a.access_time) AS first_used,
    MAX(a.access_time) AS last_used,
    SUM(CASE WHEN a.result = 'ALLOW' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS allowed_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN a.result = 'DENY' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS denied_count,
    ROUND(
        100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN a.result = 'ALLOW' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*),
        2
    ) AS allow_rate_pct
FROM access_log_facts a
WHERE a.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'
GROUP BY
    a.permission,
    a.action,
    a.result
ORDER BY
    usage_count DESC,
    a.permission,
    a.action
